w04.title: Electrical Kitchen Appliances & Conservation of Energy
w04.topic: Conservation of energy in electrical kitchen appliances — oven, refrigerator and dishwasher.
w04.lo1: State the Law of Conservation of Energy and identify the dominant energy transformation in each appliance.
w04.lo2: Use the formulas P = V × I and E = P × t to calculate power and energy.
w04.lo3: Distinguish resistive loads (cos φ = 1) from motor/compressor loads (cos φ < 1).
w04.lo4: Calculate the cost of running an appliance for a given time at a given electricity tariff.
w04.resources: Calculator, electricity tariff (e.g. 0.30 €/kWh), appliance nameplate data (or manufacturer website).

cons.title: Conservation of Energy
cons.body: The Law of Conservation of Energy states that energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed. In kitchen appliances:
